purpose: Trigger-behavior spec, two consumers: (1) CI runs tools/run_trigger_evals.py against these cases lexically; (2) after any description change, verify by hand in a fresh agent session. Cases with lexical:false trigger via reasoning, not vocabulary, and are covered by the behavioral tier (evals.json).

cases.prompt: this task is way too big for one pass, can you orchestrate it across a team of models and run the subtasks in parallel?
cases.assert: Activates; frames deliverable + success criteria before any dispatch; plans waves for parallel workers.

cases.prompt: fan this out: research all 12 competitors in parallel with cheap workers and have a stronger model review the plan first
cases.assert: Activates; uses stateless worker briefs with inline inputs; mandatory advisor plan review happens before dispatch.

cases.prompt: run the advisor worker loop on this project brief, I want one model orchestrating and an advisor model checking the plan
cases.assert: Activates; follows the 7-step loop; consults the advisor at plan review and taste pass only.

cases.prompt: my test suite is slow, run the tests in parallel across more CI workers
cases.assert: Does not trigger; CI test parallelism is infra config, not multi-model orchestration.

cases.prompt: which model should I use for my chatbot, something cheap or something strong?
cases.assert: Does not trigger; picking one model for an app is not running a model team.

cases.prompt: review this one file and fix the bug in the parser
cases.assert: Does not trigger; single-pass single-model work. The skill's own scope line excludes it.
